notes: Antigravity foreground subagent, UNBLOCKED by subagent linking (antigravity_subagent.go). The parent spawns ONE subagent via define_subagent + invoke_subagent; the child runs as its OWN top-level conversation brain/<child>/, discovered as its own session (transcript_new). AntigravityParentConvID scans sibling brain transcripts for the parent whose INVOKE_SUBAGENT step references the child's conversationId and the detector emits parent_linked (helpers.go:102). SubagentCount(parent)=1 while the child is alive; reevaluateParent holds the parent working until the child ends; parent settles working→ready only after the last child ends. The child runs inside the parent's invoke and has no own agy process, so the child session stays pid=0 (transcript-first, first-class) — assert ParentSessionID linkage + subagent summary, NOT a child PID. Single-birth transcript-first adapter for both parent and child (session id off the <conv-id> dir, no proc-<PID> presession). Requires a capable model (Gemini 3.1 Pro High / 3.5 Flash) — GPT-OSS 120B fumbles the schema. Model harvested from <USER_SETTINGS_CHANGE> ('Gemini 3.1 Pro' on this account). Tokens/cost NOT in the antigravity JSONL.

text: Parent conv-id session appears in ready when transcript.jsonl is written (single-birth, no proc-<PID> presession)
The spawn prompt drives the parent ready → working
The subagent's own conversation transcript materializes at brain/<child>/.system_generated/logs/transcript.jsonl — fswatcher discovers a new SessionState for the child

AntigravityParentConvID resolves the parent (the sibling conversation whose INVOKE_SUBAGENT step references the child conv-id); detector emits parent_linked binding child.session_id → parent.session_id (ParentSessionID = parent.session_id). SubagentCount(parent)=1 while the child is alive.

invariants: no state_transition to ready for parent
no state_transition to waiting for parent
text: Parent stays working while the child is alive — reevaluateParent holds it on hasActiveChildren
After the child reports back and ends, the parent transitions working → ready (only after the last child ends)
